title: Fragment Stitch Archive
hypothesis: Fragment Stitch Archive tests whether archive recovery can be felt through physical reconstruction of torn document pieces rather than abstract custody transfers or filing operations.
dataModel: seeded torn fragments with jagged SVG edges and partial text; mutation:fragment-stitch
recordCountStrategy: seeded variable 7-22 initial fragments that combine into 3-14 recovered records based on stitch count
interactionModel: drag fragments near matching torn edges to stitch them together; stitched groups merge into recovered records, reduce fragment count, update SVG paths, and persist stitch state in localStorage
stateModel: localStorage stores stitched fragment groupings, recovered record ids with merged text, and fragment positions; stitches are irreversible
visualLanguage: torn paper edges with ragged SVG paths, partial text on jagged shapes, warm decay tones, visible stitch marks at join lines, evidence of physical damage
expectedStructuralChange: Interaction must merge SVG paths at stitch points, reduce total fragment count, create new compound records from combined pieces, update visible text content, and persist irreversible stitch state across sessions.
